architecture_summary: The paper proposes a novel Transformer relevancy propagation method based on Deep Taylor Decomposition and LRP, then integrates relevance and gradients through attention layers and skip connections to produce class-specific visualizations for self-attention models.

benchmark_results.table_caption: Positive and Negative perturbation AUC results (percents) for the predicted and target classes, on the ImageNet [31] validation set. For positive perturbation lower is better, and for negative perturbation higher is better.

novel_contributions: a novel way to compute relevancy for Transformer networks
a relevancy propagation rule applicable to both positive and negative attributions
a normalization term for non-parametric layers, such as add and matrix multiplication
a class-specific visualization method for self-attention models
benchmarking on visual Transformer networks and a text classification problem

key_claims.claim: The paper introduces a new Transformer interpretability method that propagates relevance through attention layers and skip connections while maintaining total relevancy across layers.
The method is class-specific by construction and is presented as the only Transformer visualization method with this property, as far as the authors can ascertain.
The method is evaluated on ImageNet validation, ImageNet-Segmentation, and the Movie Reviews benchmark from ERASER.
On ImageNet perturbation tests, the method achieves the best AUC among the compared explainability methods.
On ImageNet-Segmentation, the method outperforms all baselines on pixel accuracy, mAP, and mIoU.
On the Movie Reviews rationale extraction task, the method consistently outperforms the baselines across top-k token settings.
The ablation study shows that combining relevance, gradients, and rollout contributes to the strongest performance.
